reasoning: The stock currently trades around $113.66 on the forecast date (May 11, 2025). The baseline statistical analysis via historical prices indicates short-term probabilities near 50%, with long-term upward drift typically pushing longer-horizon predictions higher. However, GPC declared a $1.03 quarterly dividend (approx. 0.9% of the share price) with an ex-dividend date of June 6, 2025. This will result in an automatic mechanical drop in the share price right before the June 10 resolution date, thereby providing a headwind that pushes the June 10 probability slightly below 0.5. For the August and November resolution dates, the longer-term market drift (assumed 8-10% annualized) is expected to offset the dividend-induced drops (including another likely dividend in early September), pushing the likelihood of being above the May 11 price slightly higher, into the low-to-mid 50s. Adhering to the guideline that remote horizon uncertainties pull probabilities close to 0.5, all estimates are placed very near 0.5.
